- Classes instantiating this MAY compose other microschemas via attributes

abstract: true

docs.title: Slot: absolute_time
docs.text: Calendar date when a measurement was taken

docs.title: Slot: activity_type
docs.text: Activity that adds context to a clinical measurement

docs.title: Slot: age_at_condition_end
docs.text: Age of the subject at the end of the condition

docs.title: Slot: age_at_condition_record
docs.text: Age of participant when record of the condition was collected. This slot can accommodate an "age at encounter" where a medical history was collected, or a record was adjudicated.

docs.title: Slot: age_at_condition_start
docs.text: Age of the subject at the start of the condition

docs.title: Slot: age_at_drug_end
docs.text: Age of the subject at the end of drug exposure

docs.title: Slot: age_at_drug_record
docs.text: _Age of participant when record of the drug was collected. This slot can accommodate an "age at encounter" where the participant was asked for their medication list. _

docs.title: Slot: age_at_drug_start
docs.text: Age of the subject at the start of drug exposure

docs.title: Slot: age_at_measurement
docs.text: Age of the subject at the time of measurement

docs.title: Slot: age_at_procedure_end
docs.text: Age of the subject at the end of the procedure

docs.title: Slot: age_at_procedure_record
docs.text: Age of participant when record of the procedure was collected. This slot can accommodate an "age at encounter" where the participant was asked for their medical history.

docs.title: Slot: age_at_procedure_start
docs.text: Age of the subject at the start of the procedure

docs.title: Slot: alert_value
docs.text: List of values that provide extra optional information about a variable. Often this is used to indicate a violation of QA/QC

docs.title: Slot: associated_evidence
docs.text: The method used for diagnosis

docs.title: Slot: associated_study
docs.text: The study that produced the variable data

docs.title: bdc-variable-library
docs.text:
LinkML models for variables in the BDC Knowledge Library

URI: https://w3id.org/linkml/bdc-variable-library

docs.title: Slot: bdchm_type
docs.text: Entity type from BDCHM that defines the variable type

docs.title: Slot: body_location
docs.text: A CURIE from UBERON

docs.title: Slot: body_position
docs.text: The body position during measurement

docs.title: Slot: calculated_from
docs.text: An expression pointing at other CDE classes

docs.title: Slot: cde_id
docs.text: CURIE from Condor

docs.title: Slot: code
docs.text: The code value as a CURIE

docs.title: Slot: code_label
docs.text: Human-readable label for the code

docs.title: Slot: code_system
docs.text: The code system URI

docs.title: Slot: coded_values
docs.text: Pipe-delimited string of allowable values. Should only be used if the data_type is enum. Corresponds to VALUES field in dbGAP

docs.title: Slot: collected_by
docs.text: Who collected the measurement

docs.title: Slot: comment
docs.text: Any text comment provided by the original data provider. Corresponds to the COMMENT field in dbGAP

docs.title: Slot: comparator
docs.text: Comparison operator (less than, greater than, etc.) or exact if null

docs.title: Slot: concept_type
docs.text: CURIE describing the main content of the variable. This can be from OBA, OMOP, or LOINC

docs.title: Slot: condition_provenance
docs.text: A value representing the provenance of the Condition record.

docs.title: Slot: condition_severity
docs.text: A subjective assessment of the severity of the condition.

docs.title: Slot: condition_status
docs.text: A value indicating whether the medical condition described in this record is present, absent, historically present, or unknown for this individual patient.

docs.title: Slot: condition_type
docs.text: A CURIE from MONDO or HPO

docs.title: Slot: context
docs.text: The context of the measurement

docs.title: Slot: data_type
docs.text: The data type of the observation

docs.title: Slot: datetime
docs.text: Absolute timestamp

docs.title: Slot: domain_of_use
docs.text: Domains where this microschema is applicable

docs.title: Slot: dose
docs.text: Amount of drug per administration

docs.title: Slot: drug_type
docs.text: A CURIE from RxNorm

docs.title: Slot: duration
docs.text: Duration as alternative to start/end

docs.title: Slot: file_id
docs.text: Identifier used by the organization providing the variable to BDC to uniquely identify the file or data table in their system. In most cases this will be a pht value

docs.title: Slot: file_name
docs.text: Name of file containing variable data

docs.title: Slot: frequency
docs.text: How often e.g. 2 per day

docs.title: Slot: id
docs.text: A unique identifier for a variable within BDC

docs.title: Slot: indication
docs.text: Condition/reason for drug (SNOMED, ICD, Mondo, HPO, etc)

docs.title: Slot: indicator_char
docs.text: Sentinel value used to add information to a datum or indicate a missing datum

docs.title: Slot: indicator_meaning
docs.text: Meaning of the indicator character verbatim from original data provider when possible

docs.title: Slot: instrument
docs.text: The instrument used for the measurement

docs.title: Slot: integrates
docs.text: List of variables combined in the IntegratedVariable

docs.title: Slot: interval_end
docs.text: End of the interval

docs.title: Slot: interval_start
docs.text: Start of the interval

docs.title: Slot: location
docs.text: Spatial metadata specifying where the observation was made - geolocation, place name, site id, environment, or biome

docs.title: Slot: lower_limit_normal
docs.text: placeholder

docs.title: Slot: maximum_value
docs.text: The highest value present in the data for this variable. Corresponds to the MAX field in dbGAP

docs.title: Slot: measurement_type
docs.text: A CURIE from OBA, OMOP, or LOINC

docs.title: Slot: measurement_value
docs.text: The "value" in the observation key/value pair

docs.title: Slot: metadata
docs.text: List of variable identifiers providing metadata for a compound variable

docs.title: Slot: method
docs.text: The method used for the measurement

docs.title: Slot: methodology
docs.text: Information about how an observation was made - method, instrument, reagent kit, statistical modifier

docs.title: Slot: microschema_slot
docs.text: The slot that the variable identified in the variable path should go

docs.title: Slot: minimum_value
docs.text: The lowest value present in the data for this variable. Corresponds to the MIN field in dbGAP

docs.title: Slot: missing_value
docs.text: Sentinel value to indicate a missing value or other data nuance

docs.title: Slot: observation_result
docs.text: The outcome of the observation type - answer, value, result, determination

docs.title: Slot: observation_type
docs.text: Question being asked, measurement being taken, quality being observed

docs.title: Slot: offset
docs.text: Offset from event (e.g., "+3 days")

docs.title: Slot: percent_predicted_value
docs.text: Ratio of the actual measured value to the predicted value

docs.title: Slot: predicted_value
docs.text: A predicted measurement value based on a reference population, range, equation, or other relevant information. Often used to normalize a measurement based on body size, age, or sex

docs.title: Slot: procedure_type
docs.text: A CURIE from OMOP

docs.title: Slot: profile_version
docs.text: Version of microschema profile this conforms to

docs.title: Slot: quantity_unit
docs.text: Unit (UCUM, UO, QUDT, etc.)

docs.title: Slot: quantity_value
docs.text: The numeric value

docs.title: Slot: reagent_kit
docs.text: The reagent kit used for the measurement

docs.title: Slot: relationship_to_participant
docs.text: A value indicating the relationship between the Participant to which the Condition is attributed and the individual who had the reported Condition. If the Condition is affecting the participant themselves, then 'Self' is the appropriate relationship.

docs.title: Slot: relative_timing
docs.text: Timing of the activity relative to the clinical measurement

docs.title: Slot: relative_to_event
docs.text: Event this is relative to

docs.title: Slot: resolution
docs.text: The number of decimal places a measurement is represented in the data. Corresponds to the RESOLUTION field in dbGAP

docs.title: Slot: route_of_administration
docs.text: Routes of drug administration

docs.title: Slot: source_id
docs.text: Identifier used by the organization providing the variable to BDC to uniquely identify the variable in their system. In most cases this will be a phv value.

docs.title: Slot: source_variable_description
docs.text: Description of the variable provided by the original data provider Corresponds to the VARDESC field in dbGAP

docs.title: Slot: study_site
docs.text: Institution name, clinic name, or other indication of where a measurement was taken geographically

docs.title: Slot: subject
docs.text: Entity being measured, observed, or interviewed - person, animal, specimen, or environmental material

docs.title: Slot: subject_age
docs.text: Age of the subject at this timepoint (e.g., age when the event occurred)

docs.title: Slot: subject_identifier
docs.text: An identifier for the subject of the datum

docs.title: Slot: temporality
docs.text: Temporal metadata specifying when an observation was made. This can be relative or absolute - datetime, age of person, season, or geologic era

docs.title: Slot: unit
docs.text: A unit from UCUM

docs.title: Slot: upper_limit_normal
docs.text: placeholder

docs.title: Slot: variable_description
docs.text: Human readable description of the variable.

docs.title: Slot: variable_label
docs.text: Human readable label describing the variable

docs.title: Slot: variable_name
docs.text: The name of the variable according to the original data provider Corresponds to the VARNAME field in dbGAP
